metadata.title: Points to Consider Concerning Endpoints in Clinical Studies with Haematopoietic Growth Factors for Mobilisation of Autologous Stem Cells

rules.rule_text: The rationale for all aspects of stem cell transplantation must be strictly defined by the applicant, including the substance/combination used for mobilisation, dosage and schedule, the collection procedure, and the procedure for surveying cell numbers.
rules.source_section: 2.3 Mobilisation, collection and preparation of stem cells
rules.verbatim_quote: The rationale for all aspects of stem cell transplantation must be strictly defined by the applicant, regarding the substance/combination used for mobilisation, the dosage and schedule of each substance within the regimen, the collection procedure and the procedure for surveying cell numbers.

rules.rule_text: CD34+ cell measurement methods must be based on current standards as recommended by relevant societies (e.g. ISHAGE guidelines for CD34+ cell determination by flow cytometry).
rules.source_section: 2.3 Mobilisation, collection and preparation of stem cells
rules.verbatim_quote: This has to be based on current standards and as recommended by relevant societies or respective literature. Note: Methods for standardisation of CD34+ cells have been published e.g. by the International Society for Hematology and Graft Engineering [Sutherland et al., The ISHAGE guidelines for CD34+ cell determination by flow cytometry

rules.rule_text: In multicentre evaluations of stem cells or CD34+ cells, reproducibility studies of all parts of the stem cell preparation procedure must accompany the clinical efficacy evaluation, particularly methods to determine CD34+ cell quality and number.
rules.source_section: 2.3 Mobilisation, collection and preparation of stem cells
rules.verbatim_quote: As long as multicentre evaluations of stem cells or CD34+ cells are performed, studies on the reproducibility of all parts of the stem cell preparation procedure, in particular of methods to determine the quality and number of CD34+ cells, must accompany an evaluation of the clinical efficacy of a particular substance on stem cell yield.

rules.rule_text: Patients should be homogeneous concerning basic diseases. If patients with different basic diseases are included, the rationale must be provided and individual studies on a specific disease should be sufficiently powered to allow direct conclusions.
rules.verbatim_quote: As far as possible, patients should be homogenous concerning basic diseases, since they might differ substantially in marrow infiltration, contaminating neoplastic cells, myelosuppressive stem cell toxic pre-treatment and previous radiotherapy. In cases where patients with different basic diseases are included the rationale should be provided. Individual studies on a specific disease should be sufficiently powered to allow direct conclusions.

rules.rule_text: Retrospective meta-analysis of pooled data without a priori stratification is discouraged.
rules.verbatim_quote: Retrospective meta-analysis of pooled data without a priori stratification is discouraged.

rules.rule_text: Patients who have failed previous attempts to mobilise and collect sufficient stem cells by standard means are extremely valuable for determining potential efficacy of a new cytokine/substance and should be studied.
rules.source_section: 3.1 Treatment of patients with previously established failure for stem cell mobilisation
rules.verbatim_quote: Patients having failed previous attempts to mobilise and collect a sufficient number of stem cells, allowing them to proceed to transplantation by standard means (in particular by a standard cytokine or a combination of a standard cytokine plus chemotherapy), are extremely valuable for determining the potential efficacy of a new cytokine/substance.

rules.rule_text: For patients at risk of poor mobilisation, the applicant must justify the criteria used to define increased risk, including degree of marrow infiltration, age, number and type of prior treatment regimens.
rules.source_section: 3.2 Treatment of patients who are at risk for poor mobilisation
rules.verbatim_quote: Under certain circumstances - which have to be justified by the applicant - it may be possible to define patients with an a priori increased risk of poor mobilisation or mobilisation failure because of the underlying neoplastic disease (degree of marrow infiltration), age, number and type of prior treatment regimens (distribution of weighted risk factors).

rules.rule_text: For standard/low risk patients where improved toxicity is the primary endpoint, sufficient evidence for a beneficial risk/benefit ratio must exist prior to study and be justified by the applicant.
rules.source_section: 3.3 Patients with a standard and low risk for poor mobilisation
rules.verbatim_quote: Sufficient evidence for a beneficial risk/benefit ratio must exist prior to study and must be justified by the applicant.

rules.rule_text: Phase II studies should ideally include a control arm with a standard regimen to obtain preliminary evidence of relative efficacy and safety of standard and new approaches.
rules.verbatim_quote: Ideally, the study should include a control arm with a standard regimen in order to get preliminary evidence of the relative efficacy and safety of the standard and new approaches.

rules.rule_text: Phase II study population must include homogeneous groups of patients who are candidates for stem cell transplantation, mobilised with HGF with or without chemotherapy.
rules.verbatim_quote: Homogeneous groups of patients, who are candidates for stem cell transplantation. Groups of patients mobilised with HGF +/- chemotherapy (as justified by the applicant) should be investigated.

rules.rule_text: Whenever possible, studies should be randomised for all populations (failed mobilisation, at risk for poor mobilisation, standard risk).
rules.verbatim_quote: Whenever possible, studies should be randomised for all populations mentioned above.

rules.rule_text: The primary endpoint in Phase II should be the percentage of patients achieving the minimal CD34+ cell number (>= 2x10^6/kg) considered necessary and safe to proceed to autotransplantation, or an adequate toxicity marker in the inferior toxicity situation.
rules.verbatim_quote: Usually the percentage of patients achieving the minimal (=2x10^6/kg) number of CD34+ cells considered necessary and safe to proceed to autotransplantation or an adequate toxicity marker (which has to be selected according to the toxicity profile of the substances/regimens compared) in the inferior toxicity situation will be considered the primary endpoint of the study.
rules.exceptions: Other options concerning the number of CD34+ cells have to be justified by the applicant.

rules.rule_text: Phase II secondary endpoints must include biomarkers for clinical efficacy (optimal CD34+ dose of 5x10^6/kg or target dose as defined by applicant), number of aphereses, and number of CD34+ cells/kg/apheresis.
rules.verbatim_quote: Biomarkers for clinical efficacy (optimal dose of CD34+ cells [i.e. 5x10^6/kg] or target dose of CD34+ cells as defined by the applicant, number of aphereses or large volume aphereses necessary to obtain the minimal number of CD34+ cells necessary for autotransplantation, number of CD34+ cells/kg/apheresis)

rules.rule_text: The number of patients proceeding to autotransplantation must be recorded as a secondary endpoint, with detailed reasons listed for any patients not autografted (including disease progression).
rules.verbatim_quote: the number of patients proceeding to autotransplantation (any reasons why patients were not autografted, including progression of their malignant disease, etc., must be listed)

rules.rule_text: Engraftment endpoints must include number of patients reaching engraftment of ANC or platelets, number becoming transfusion-independent, and time to ANC > 0.5 x 10^9/L and thrombocytes > 20 and > 50 x 10^9/L with transfusion independence.
rules.verbatim_quote: the number of patients reaching engraftment of absolute neutrophil count (ANC) or platelets, the number of patients becoming transfusion-independent, time to ANC >0.5 x 10^9/l and thrombocytes >20 and >50 x 10^9/l with independence from transfusions

rules.rule_text: Safety parameters must be reported separately for the mobilisation/collection phase and the post-reinfusion phase of stem cells.
rules.verbatim_quote: In general, adverse reactions or side effects should be reported separately for both the phase in which stem cells are being collected and the time after reinfusion of stem cells.

rules.rule_text: Safety reporting must include standard toxicity parameters, adverse reactions of the mobilisation procedure (thromboses, bleedings, central line complications) analysed by number and type of aphereses, and the number of days with fever and IV antibiotics.
rules.verbatim_quote: standard toxicity parameters, adverse reactions or side effects of the mobilisation procedure (thromboses, bleedings, central line complications), analysed according to the number and type of aphereses performed, the number of days with fever and iv-antibiotics

rules.rule_text: Phase III trials must be randomised clinical trials. A placebo-alone arm cannot be used when a standard regimen exists.
rules.verbatim_quote: Randomised clinical trials are necessary. Since a standard regimen exists, it is recognised that a placebo-alone arm cannot be used.

rules.rule_text: The applicant must provide sufficient rationale and information on the type, schedule, and dose of comparator used, which should comprise the regimen considered most efficient for the patient population.
rules.verbatim_quote: The applicant should provide sufficient rationale and information on the type, schedule and dose of comparator used, which should comprise the regimen considered most efficient for the situation of the patient.

rules.rule_text: When cytokine combinations are compared with standard cytokine alone, and higher doses of the standard could achieve increased efficacy, applicant must justify why higher dose of standard alone is not used as comparator.
rules.verbatim_quote: In case cytokine combinations (new cytokine + standard cytokine) are compared with a standard cytokine alone and if there is evidence from the literature that a higher dose of the standard cytokine alone leads to an increased efficacy as compared with the standard dose, reasons should be given why such an increased dosage of the standard cytokine alone might not serve as a comparator in terms of efficacy or why an unfavourable risk/benefit ratio may be expected from such an approach.

rules.rule_text: The Phase III primary endpoint must demonstrate a difference in the clinically most relevant outcome: the number of patients proceeding to and successfully completing autotransplantation, with detailed reasons for non-achievement. An adequate toxicity marker is also required as co-primary endpoint in the inferior toxicity situation.
rules.verbatim_quote: A difference in the clinically most relevant and therefore primary endpoint must be demonstrated, i.e. the number of patients proceeding to and successfully completing autotransplantation (with any reasons why patients do not achieve this goal being listed in detail) and an adequate toxicity marker (which has to be selected according to the toxicity profile of the substances/regimens compared) in the inferior toxicity situation.

rules.rule_text: Phase III secondary endpoints must include the percentage of patients becoming transfusion-independent, time to ANC > 0.5 x 10^9/L and thrombocytes > 20 and > 50 x 10^9/L, and hospitalisation rates and duration.
rules.verbatim_quote: the percentage of patients becoming transfusion-independent, time to ANC >0.5 x 10^9/l and thrombocytes >20 and >50 x 10^9/l with independence from transfusions, hospitalisation rates and duration

rules.rule_text: CD34+ cell numbers (minimal >= 2x10^6/kg per ISHAGE/EBMT JACIE standards, optimal 5x10^6/kg) serve as supportive biomarker evidence for efficacy, not as the primary Phase III endpoint.
rules.verbatim_quote: Biomarkers will be considered as supportive evidence for efficacy, such as: the number of patients achieving or failing minimal CD34+ cell numbers (usually >=2x10^6/kg, adopted by the ISHAGE/EBMT Joint Accreditation Committee, JACIE standards D4.132), optimal CD34+ cell numbers (usually 5x10^6/kg) or target CD34+ cell numbers

rules.rule_text: For hospitalisation endpoint in multicentre/pooled studies, the procedures and directions for hospitalisation or outpatient care must be defined a priori.
rules.verbatim_quote: hospitalisation rates and duration (concerning this endpoint, the procedures and directions for hospitalisation or outpatient care should be given a priori, particularly when multicentre studies are performed or data from studies are pooled which were carried out at different institutions or in different countries)

rules.rule_text: Post-marketing data must include long-term follow-up on disease-free survival, relapse-free survival, overall survival, long-term adverse effects (stimulation of pre-neoplastic/neoplastic clones, MDS, secondary leukaemias, secondary solid malignancies), and long-term effects on bone marrow function.
rules.source_section: 4.2.7 To be reported as post-marketing data
rules.verbatim_quote: effects on disease-free survival, relapse-free survival, overall survival in both arms or relevant adequately-matched comparator groups, long-term adverse effects like stimulation of pre-neoplastic/fully neoplastic clones (i.e. myelodysplastic syndromes, acute secondary leukaemias, secondary solid malignancies), long-term effects on bone marrow function
